publication_title: Systemic Inflammasome and Neutrophil Activation Signatures Define Early Immune Dysregulation in Treatment-Naïve Bullous Pemphigoid

biological_process_synthesis.text: The DEG signature in PBMCs from newly diagnosed bullous pemphigoid patients reveals a dominant innate immune and granulocyte-driven inflammatory program. Inflammasome components (AIM2, CASP1), DAMP molecules (S100A8, S100A9, S100A12), and Fc-receptor signaling elements suggest systemic priming toward autoantibody-mediated tissue injury. Concurrent upregulation of neutrophil effector genes (MMP8, LTF, CEACAM8, ARG1) indicates enhanced degranulation and proteolytic capacity relevant to blister formation. Immune-metabolic regulators such as IDO1 and ARG1 point to metabolic rewiring that may modulate adaptive immunity. Together, these data suggest that bullous pemphigoid involves systemic myeloid activation with inflammasome-dependent amplification of autoimmune inflammation.

statements: PBMC inflammasome activation precedes and potentiates skin blister formation in bullous pemphigoid.
Circulating neutrophils in early bullous pemphigoid are transcriptionally primed for enhanced degranulation, directly contributing to dermal-epidermal junction damage.
Upregulated FCGR1A enhances immune complex-driven systemic cytokine amplification in early disease.
IDO1 upregulation reflects a compensatory immune suppression mechanism that fails to restrain pathogenic inflammation.
ALOX12-mediated lipid signaling contributes to recruitment and activation of granulocytes in bullous pemphigoid.
A strong systemic inflammasome signature in PBMCs suggests bullous pemphigoid is not solely a skin-localized disease.
A prominent CEBPE-driven neutrophil lineage transcriptional program indicates granulocyte developmental skewing in circulation.
Concurrent activation markers (CASP1, S100A8/A9) and regulatory checkpoints (IDO1, IL1R2) imply a dysregulated inflammatory feedback loop.
Integration of Fc receptor signaling with DAMP-driven sterile inflammation suggests immune complex-triggered amplification mechanisms.

modules.associated_phenotype: T cell suppression and metabolic rewiring

summaries.summary: GO enrichment highlights strong overrepresentation of innate immune activation, neutrophil degranulation, inflammasome assembly, cytokine production, and leukocyte migration. These findings indicate systemic activation of granulocyte-driven inflammatory programs in PBMCs from treatment-naïve bullous pemphigoid patients.

KEGG pathway themes converge on Toll-like receptor signaling, NOD-like receptor signaling, cytokine-cytokine receptor interaction, Fc gamma receptor signaling, and apoptosis. These pathways center on NF-κB activation and IL-1 family cytokine maturation, supporting immune complex-driven amplification of inflammation.

Reactome-level interpretation emphasizes inflammasome assembly, neutrophil granule protein release, immune complex signaling, and immune-metabolic regulation. The network architecture suggests coordinated innate immune priming with incomplete regulatory counterbalance in early bullous pemphigoid.
